signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD 1W momentum (+1.403%) vs CHF laggard status (-0.795%) creates a 2.198% G10 spread that favors upside continuation. The RBNZ hiking bias and +250bp OIS carry advantage over CHF drive the divergence despite NZD COT net short -25.7k. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 46% hike priced) on 2 September is the catalyst for confirming the hawkish path.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Fed Inflation Warning + Carry Advantage
signals.text: JPY COT net short -137.8k (74.7% short) remains the dominant G10 positioning extreme, leaving the pair vulnerable to a squeeze if the 161.67 Friday close is tested. The Federal Reserve's Friday report citing high inflation due to tariffs reinforces the +260bp USD OIS carry advantage and limits JPY recovery. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 July determines if the yield gap begins to compress.

signals.title: AUD/USD — China Reflation Gap + Carry Support
signals.text: AUD COT net long +21.5k (62.1% long) aligns with a +77bp OIS carry advantage to support the 0.6955 Friday close. Slowing Chinese CPI at 1.0% introduces a reflation gap that weighs on the USD side of the pair while AUD maintains a moderate positive CESI bias. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 August is the catalyst for sustaining the current yield advantage.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CESI Outperformance + Trump Ceasefire Shock
signals.text: GBP COT net long +9.7k and a positive CESI (+22) support the 1.3398 Friday close as risk appetite stabilizes following weekend geopolitical headlines. The -15bp OIS carry advantage for GBP and restrictive +0.78% real rate provide a fundamental floor against USD strength. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 30 July is the catalyst for resolving the current neutral market bias.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Employment Beat vs COT Crowding
signals.text: CAD COT net short -92.8k (79.4% short) creates significant short-squeeze risk following a June employment beat of +18.2K jobs. While the +130bp OIS carry advantage favors USD, the extreme retail short bias (80%) and negative CAD CESI (-9) suggest the 1.4153 level is sensitive to data surprises. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 15 July is the catalyst for a directional shift.
